course_title: Head and Neck Anatomy for Dentistry

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate knowledge of the major anatomical structures of the head and neck and relate this to their function.
Demonstrate in depth knowledge of the anatomical structures of the orofacial region, as relates to the normal function of the mouth and associated structures.
Demonstrate in depth knowledge of the histological and radiographic features of structures of the orofacial region.
Differentiate between normal and abnormal features (pathology) with respect to structures of the oro-facial region and appreciate their clinical relevance to dental practice.
Apply knowledge and appreciate the importance of head and neck anatomy, oral biology and oral histology for the practice of dentistry within a comprehensive patient care model.

assessments.task_description:
The in-semester exam will be held in-person and invigilated via Inspera and centrally administered by the university during the in-semester period.

Students will ideally bring their own laptops with the Inspera safe browser installed to complete and submit this exam. If a student does not have a laptop with access to the internet (connected to Eduroam), the student may be able to borrow a laptop from UQ Library (https://web.library.uq.edu.au/library-and-student-it-help/using-library-devices/laptop-loans).

This 60-minute exam might contain a combination of multiple-choice, essay, short-answer and problem-solving questions covering content delivered across all sessions from Week 1 to Week 5.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Students will rotate through various anatomical stations and answer questions based on labelled anatomical models/specimens at each station. 

Each station will have a time limit of 60 seconds, and the student moves on to the next station when the time limit is reached. 

The exam will be of 60 minutes in total duration, including the time allocated for general instructions. 

The exam will start at 09:00 am and finish at 12:00 pm at the Gross Anatomy Facility at the St Lucia Campus.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
This exam will be held in-person and invigilated via Inspera, and students will complete and submit this using their own device as per the in-semester exam. This 90-minute exam might contain a combination of multiple-choice, essay, short-answer and problem-solving questions covering content delivered across all sessions from Week 1 to Week 13.

This is a Must Pass assessment item (achieve a minimum of 60% to pass).

learning_activities.topic:
GAF Induction Modules

Online Anatomy Induction Training: Students must complete the online training for the "SBMS Code of Conduct for use of Donated Human Tissues" and the "Online GAF induction" at the UQ blackboard training website prior to attending their first in-person induction session. A record of student completions of this training will be cross-checked against class rolls for each practical session.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05

A record of student completions of this training will be cross-checked against class rolls for each practical session.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Lectures on head and neck anatomy

A series of lectures covering macrostructures and microstructures of the head and neck relevant to clinical practice. Attendance in all learning activities is highly recommended.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Lectures on head and neck anatomy A series of lectures covering macrostructures and microstructures of the head and neck relevant to clinical practice. Attendance in all learning activities is highly recommended.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 13(03 Aug - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Practical sessions (Workshops)

A combination of group activities and digital anatomy sessions will be delivered. These sessions are neither recorded nor repeated; hence, attendance is highly encouraged.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Practical sessions (Workshops) A combination of group activities and digital anatomy sessions will be delivered. These sessions are neither recorded nor repeated; hence, attendance is highly encouraged.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical sessions (GAF)

Gross Anatomy practical sessions will be delivered as compulsory, non-repeated sessions; attendance is mandatory.

Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) & Identification: It is a requirement that ALL students wear a clean white laboratory coat (or disposable gown for dissection classes as directed), safety glasses, fully enclosed shoes and their student identification cards when in the Gross Anatomy Facility. Students WILL NOT be permitted in the laboratory (including their first laboratory session) without these items. PPE and identification card holders can be purchased from anywhere. Each student must have their UQ Student Identification Card visibly displayed above the waist at all times during these sessions.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
